---
id: prd-00020-checkout-redesign
type: prd
role: main
status: active
parent: idea-00030-checkout-foundations
---

# One-line Summary

Improve guest checkout completion while preserving enough control for fraud review and order communication.

# Vision & Goals

Reduce checkout friction for first-time shoppers without increasing operational risk.

# Actor

Guest shoppers and operations analysts.

# In Scope

Guest checkout, risk review routing, and confirmation messaging.

# Out of Scope

Payment processor migration, loyalty accounts, and returns flows.

# Functional Requirements

- [ ] FR-01: Let a shopper place an order without creating an account.
- [ ] FR-02: Route high-risk guest checkout orders into a manual review queue before capture.
- [ ] FR-03: Send a confirmation email immediately after a successful guest checkout.

# User Experience

Keep the checkout short, make the review path invisible to low-risk shoppers, and provide clear order confirmation.

# Risks

Manual review latency could reduce conversion if thresholds are too aggressive.

# Dependencies

Payment gateway risk signals and the transactional email service.

item.text: The ambiguity is the handoff between `FR-02` and `FR-03`: whether a high-risk guest order should get a true confirmation immediately or only after review. I’m treating this patch as a clarification that low-risk orders get immediate confirmation, while review-routed orders get an immediate pending-status email and only receive confirmation after approval.

item.aggregated_output:
---
id: story-00020-fr-03-guest-checkout-confirmation-email-patch
type: user-story
role: patch
status: active
parent: prd-00020-checkout-redesign
requirement: FR-03
---

# Summary

Clarify the guest checkout confirmation email flow when manual review is triggered.

# User Story

As a guest shopper, I want the email I receive after checkout to match my order status so that I know whether my order is confirmed or still pending review.

# Acceptance Criteria

- Given a guest checkout order that is approved during checkout, when the order is placed successfully, then the system sends the standard confirmation email immediately.
- Given a guest checkout order that is routed into the manual review queue from `FR-02`, when checkout is submitted successfully, then the system sends an immediate pending-review email instead of the standard confirmation email.
- Given a manually reviewed guest checkout order that is later approved, when the order is cleared to proceed after review, then the system sends the standard confirmation email at that point.
- Given a manually reviewed guest checkout order that is declined, then the system does not send the standard confirmation email.

# Notes

This patch aligns `FR-03` with `FR-02` by separating "order received and pending review" from "order confirmed."

item.text:
Added the patch user story at [story-00020-fr-03-guest-checkout-confirmation-email-patch.md](/Users/ryan/GitHubProjects/ryan-alexander-zhang/toad-copilot/skills/toad-copilot/writing-user-story-workspace/iteration-1/eval-3-patch-user-story/without_skill/run-1/project/docs/user-stories/story-00020-fr-03-guest-checkout-confirmation-email-patch.md:1).

It clarifies the `FR-02`/`FR-03` handoff: approved guest orders get the standard confirmation email immediately, review-routed orders get an immediate pending-review email instead, the confirmation email is sent only after review approval, and declined reviewed orders do not receive the confirmation email.

Assumption I made explicitly: there was no existing `docs/user-stories` template in this project root, so I used PRD-style frontmatter plus a minimal patch-story structure. Verified the new file exists under `docs/user-stories`; no tests were needed for this docs-only change.
